question_title: What will be the value of "ICE BofA Euro High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread" on 2026-02-26?
summary: The ICE BofA Euro High Yield Index OAS is currently at 2.68% (Feb 16) and has been in a tight 2.56-2.70 range for the past 30 trading days. With only ~8 trading days to the resolution date (Feb 26), the forecast is anchored on the current value with uncertainty derived from Monte Carlo simulation using empirical daily volatility (~3.1-3.3 bps in recent regime, ~6.0 bps full history). The distribution is roughly symmetric with a slight right skew reflecting the fat-tailed nature of credit spread movements. The European credit environment is benign (ECB cuts expected, sovereign spreads at lows), but potential catalysts include a US Supreme Court ruling on tariff legality (Feb 20-25). The mixed simulation (50% recent regime, 20% very recent, 30% full-history with t-distribution for tail risk) produces a median of ~2.66 and relatively tight confidence intervals appropriate for this low-volatility regime.

factors.description: Current value is 2.68% (Feb 16), starting point for simulation. Spread has been in tight 2.56-2.70 range for 30+ days.
Very low recent daily volatility (~3.1 bps std over last 20 days, ~3.3 bps over 60 days) limits the range of outcomes over 8 trading days.
Slight negative drift in recent 60-day data (mean daily change: -0.004), suggesting mild downward tendency, though negligible over 8 days.
European credit environment broadly benign: ECB rate cuts expected, sovereign spreads at historic lows, credit conditions supportive. Favors stable-to-tighter spreads.
Slight recent uptick from 2.62 to 2.68-2.69 over last few days may reflect transient widening pressure.
US Supreme Court tariff ruling possible Feb 20-25, a potential catalyst for spread widening if adverse. Adds modest upside risk to spreads.
Full-history data shows positive skew (0.73) and heavy tails (kurtosis 12.3) in daily changes — credit spreads can spike sharply. Right tail properly modeled via t-distribution component.

**Key reasoning:**
- Current value: 2.68% (Feb 16), in a very tight 2.56-2.70 range for 30+ days
- Only ~8 trading days to resolution — limited scope for movement
- Recent daily volatility is very low (~3.1 bps), producing tight confidence intervals
- European credit environment is benign (ECB cuts expected, sovereign spreads at lows)
- Monte Carlo simulation using mixed distributions (recent regime + full-history tail risk with t-distribution) anchors the forecast near current levels
- Small right-tail risk from potential US Supreme Court tariff ruling (Feb 20-25) and inherent positive skew in credit spread movements
